Bring history into the classroom.

Grantham Museum loan boxes — £10 per week · eleven subjects · no deposit required

No coach. No permission slips. No wasted Friday afternoons. A beautifully prepared box of real heritage resources, ready to open on your classroom table.

Grantham Museum's loan boxes bring hands-on history directly to you. Each box arrives with teacher notes, curriculum links, and a full set of pupil activity sheets. Ideal for primary schools, home educators, youth groups, and community organisations.

Eleven boxes. Endless possibilities.

𓂀  Spotlight: Egyptians

A complete ready-to-teach resource for KS2 History

Open the box and you have everything for a full session on Ancient Egypt. Nine pupil activities take children from object observation through to myth, mummification, and writing their own name in hieroglyphics.

  • God Match — identify ten Egyptian gods from illustrated descriptions
  • Hieroglyphics — write your name in the Egyptian alphabet
  • Egyptian Mummy — colour and fold a sarcophagus craft
  • Herodotus on Mummification — a real ancient source text
  • Written Sources — primary texts from 2400 to 290 BC
  • Egyptian Gods — extended reading on cosmic and animal gods
  • Egyptian Myths — The Destruction of Mankind narrative
  • Curious Curator — record an object like a museum professional
  • I Spy — object observation and evidence skills

Fold-and-colour sarcophagus craft

Can your class match all ten gods? One of nine activities in the box.

Write your name — or send a secret message — in Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphics.

What's inside the box

Twelve real artefacts and replica objects to examine and explore — each with its own information sheet covering function, background, and the technology behind it.

Shabti Statue

A good luck charm placed with the dead to help them in the next world.

⚠️ Fragile — handle with care

Green Glazed Pot

Small pottery jars found in homes and tombs across Ancient Egypt, used for cooking or storage.

⚠️ Fragile — handle with care

Ceramic Amulet

Worn as jewellery for protection. Shaped as the Ankh — the Egyptian sign of life.

Please do not remove from box

Oyster Shell Pendant

Engraved with the name King Senwosret and the Eye of Horus. Jewellery worn by men and women alike.

Please do not remove from box

Sewn Leather Fragments

Made around 2000 BC and sewn with fine leather thong. Leather was relatively rare in Ancient Egypt.

Head of Figurine

A ceramic head probably part of a Shabti statue — a charm placed in a tomb to assist the deceased.

⚠️ Fragile — handle with care

Painted Cloth Fragment

Painted cloth fragments coated with plaster formed part of a coffin decoration. Dating to 940 to 525 BC.

Clay Sphinx

A seal in the shape of a sphinx, used to stamp papyrus documents or engrave the owner's name.

⚠️ Fragile — handle with care

Stone Head

A small carving depicting the head of an Egyptian priest, bald to maintain purity. Made from black basalt.

Linen Cloth

Much of Ancient Egyptian clothing was made from linen. This piece would have been worn as a loincloth by workers.

Mummy Cloth Wrapping

Cloth bandages used at several stages of mummification. Bodies were wrapped in many layers of linen.

Pieces of Painted Plaster

Surviving Egyptian art found in tombs describing food and belongings the dead wished to take to the afterlife.

Plus: 4 postcards · Contents sheets for each layer · Full teacher notes and background information for every object.

Or bring the classroom to the heart of history.

The loan box is just the beginning. Come and visit us at Grantham Museum — housed in a beautiful 1926 Carnegie Library right in the heart of Grantham. Admission is free; the only cost to your school is transport.

🍎

Discover Grantham's greats — Newton, Thatcher, and Edith Smith, Britain's first female police officer with powers of arrest

🏺

Explore original objects and artefacts from across history in a genuine museum setting

🏙️

See the 1950s Grantham scale model and the historic apothecary shop

📍

Free admission for all — only your transport to cover

What's available to borrow

𓂀

Egyptians

Gods, mummies, hieroglyphics and ancient artefacts. Ideal for KS2 History.

🏛

Roman Life

Roman Britain, society and daily life. Strong KS2 and KS3 curriculum links.

🎒

Victorian School

Life in a Victorian classroom — rules, slates, and discipline.

🍳

Victorian Kitchen

Food, cooking and domestic life in the Victorian home.

✍️

History of Writing

From clay tablets to printing — how writing changed the world.

📝

The Write Stuff

Creative writing and communication across history.

✈️

WW2

The Home Front, the Blitz, rationing and wartime Britain.

🧸

Toys

Play through the ages — from Victorian tops to post-war favourites.

🌍

Africa

People, culture, landscape and heritage across the African continent.

🗺️

Local Studies

Grantham's own story — people, places and local heritage.

🌊

The Seaside

A Victorian and Edwardian seaside holiday — buckets, bathing machines and beyond.
